decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing a wide variety of creatures and spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy while also applying pressure with aggressive creatures. The key concept revolves around early game disruption with cards like Thoughtseize and Swords to Plowshares, while leveraging creatures like Stoneforge Mystic and Skyclave Apparition for board presence. The inclusion of Yorion as a companion allows for additional value and card advantage. The deck aims to control the battlefield and disrupt the opponent's game plan, while steadily building towards a win condition through a mix of creatures and equipment.

This Mardu Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature strategies with powerful support cards. It utilizes a mix of efficient creatures like Ocelot Pride and Guide of Souls, along with disruptive spells like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize to disrupt opponents' game plans. The deck also includes removal spells like Swords to Plowshares to deal with opposing threats. The mana base is carefully constructed with a balance of fetch lands, dual lands, and utility lands like Karakas to ensure consistent access to all colors of mana needed for the deck's powerful spells and creatures.

This control deck focuses on utilizing a variety of spells and creatures to control the board, disrupt opponent's strategies, and ultimately win through efficient card advantage and powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Uro, Titan of Nature's Wrath. The key strategy involves a combination of card selection and disruption spells like Brainstorm, Force of Will, and Swords to Plowshares to maintain board control and outvalue the opponent. Leyline Binding and Up the Beanstalk help to further solidify the game plan and set up for powerful late-game plays.

This deck focuses on building a fast and aggressive board presence with low-cost creatures like Dauthi Voidwalker, Moonshadow, and Nethergoyf, supported by disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. Dark Ritual allows for explosive starts, while Mishra's Bauble and Urza's Bauble provide card advantage. The deck also includes some utility cards like Sheoldred's Edict and Snuff Out for removal. The mana base is designed to support the deck's aggressive strategy while also providing access to multiple colors for cards like Daze. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly with a mix of efficient creatures and disruptive spells.

The focus of this deck is to establish control over the game by using artifacts to create a prison-like effect for the opponent. By utilizing cards like Trinisphere, Urza's Saga, Karn, the Great Creator, and various key artifacts, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and limit their options. The inclusion of powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul and Ulamog provides a win condition that can quickly close out the game once control has been established. The deck is built to manipulate the game in a way that heavily favors the player using it.

The main concept of this deck is to establish control over the game by utilizing mana acceleration from cards like Birds of Paradise and Gaea's Cradle, while also disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Collector Ouphe and Wasteland. The deck aims to ramp up to powerful threats like Craterhoof Behemoth and Atraxa, Grand Unifier, and can also use Natural Order to search for key creatures. Additionally, the deck includes graveyard hate with cards like Bojuka Bog and Endurance to deal with opposing graveyard strategies. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and control elements.

The deck focuses on using powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Griselbrand to quickly overwhelm the opponent by cheating them into play with cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. A mix of control elements like Force of Will and disruptive spells like Flusterstorm help protect the combo. The goal is to ramp up quickly with cards like Lotus Petal and fast lands, while also filtering through the deck with Brainstorm and Ponder. The deck aims to win in a single explosive turn, catching the opponent off guard and securing victory.

This UWx Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win condition through card advantage and removal spells. With a mixture of cards like Brainstorm, Force of Will, and Swords to Plowshares, the deck aims to control the board and protect its key threats like Teferi, Time Raveler. By utilizing cards like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Quantum Riddler for card advantage and disruption, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent while eventually closing out the game with its threats.

This deck focuses on using artifacts and blue spells to quickly disrupt and overwhelm opponents. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and other artifact creatures provide a strong aggro presence, while Chalice of the Void and Force of Will help control the board. Urza's Saga helps to tutor for key artifacts, while Mishra's Bauble and Mox Opal provide mana ramp. The deck also includes a mix of utility artifacts like Pithing Needle and Tormod's Crypt for additional disruption. Overall, the deck aims to win quickly by combining aggressive creatures with powerful artifact synergies.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy while applying pressure with efficient creatures like Nethergoyf and Murktide Regent. Using cards like Thoughtseize and Daze to control the game early on, Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares adds a powerful finisher. Mishra's Bauble and Brainstorm help with card selection and Orcish Bowmasters can deal with troublesome creatures. With a mix of disruption and threats, this deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through tempo advantage.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly establish board presence and disrupt the opponent's game plan through a combination of efficient creatures and powerful spells. By utilizing aggressive creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Nethergoyf, along with disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Daze, the deck aims to keep the opponent on the back foot while deploying threats of its own. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Murktide Regent provide additional value and versatility to the deck, allowing for different lines of play depending on the situation.

The main idea of this deck is to aggressively play powerful creatures like Magus of the Moon and Boromir, Warden of the Tower early in the game using ramp cards like Simian Spirit Guide and Chrome Mox. By disrupting the opponent's mana base with cards like Trinisphere and Magus of the Moon, the deck aims to control the board and prevent the opponent from building up their resources. Additionally, cards like Anointed Peacekeeper and Chancellor of the Annex provide additional protection for your creatures while you close out the game with powerful attacks.

This deck aims to quickly and efficiently cheat massive creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn onto the battlefield using the card Show and Tell combined with Omniscience. By getting these powerful threats into play, the deck can overwhelm the opponent and win the game in a single turn. The deck also includes cards like Stock Up, Cunning Wish, and various counterspells to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The key strategy is to assemble the combo pieces quickly and execute the game-ending combo before the opponent can respond.

This deck aims to leverage the powerful combo of Show and Tell alongside various ways to cheat big threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Omniscience into play. It also includes key disruption pieces like Hullbreacher and Chalice of the Void to control the game and protect the combo. The deck is focused on quickly assembling its combo pieces and overwhelming the opponent with massive threats, utilizing cards like Intuition and Cunning Wish to find the necessary pieces. The goal is to establish a dominant board presence and win the game quickly and decisively.

The deck focuses on assembling the combo of Painter's Servant and Grindstone to mill the opponent's entire deck at once. It uses cards like Goblin Engineer and Goblin Welder to tutor for key pieces, while also disrupting the opponent with cards like Ensnaring Bridge and Red Elemental Blast. The deck utilizes artifacts and mana acceleration to quickly set up the combo, with support from cards like Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and Urza's Saga. Overall, the deck aims to win the game through a powerful and efficient combo strategy.

This Control - Pox deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's resources while generating value through sacrificing lands and creatures. By utilizing cards like Smallpox, Thoughtseize, and Malevolent Rumble, it aims to strip the opponent's hand and board of threats. The deck also leverages the land engine of Life from the Loam and Urza's Saga to generate card advantage and create a resource advantage over time. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis and Grist, the Hunger Tide provide alternative win conditions and ways to close out the game.
